paragraph.content: all programs within the Agency administering Federal disaster relief assistance develop and maintain proper internal management controls to prevent and detect fraud, waste, and abuse;
application databases used by the Agency to collect information on eligible recipients must record disbursements;
such tracking is designed to highlight and identify ineligible applications; and
the databases used to collect information from applications for such assistance must be integrated with disbursements and payment records.

content.p: The Administrator shall ensure that any database or similar application processing system for Federal disaster relief assistance programs administered by the Agency undergoes a review by the Inspector General of the Agency to determine the existence and implementation of such internal controls required under this section and the amendments made by this section.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: For the amendments made by this section, referred to in subsec. (b), see Codification note below.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section is comprised of section 696 of Pub. L. 109–295. Subsec. (c) of section 696 of Pub. L. 109–295 amended section 5174 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare.
